step_by_step_thinking:
Extraction of disto-angular impacted third molars typically involves surgical techniques due to their position relative to surrounding tissues like nerves and blood vessels. Potential post-extraction complications include dry socket if proper healing processes are disrupted, excessive bleeding especially given the rich vascularity of the area, and damage to adjacent soft tissues leading to slippage in the lingual sulcus but not necessarily compromising the entire ramus of the mandible.
\boxed{A}
```plaintext
Extraction of disto-angular impacted third molars typically involves surgical techniques due to their position relative to surrounding tissues like nerves and blood vessels. Based on the available information, the most likely complication mentioned in the options would be slippage in the lingual sulcus rather than extensive damage to the rami of the mandible, excessive haemorrhage being less frequent compared to dry sockets, and there's no indication supporting dry socket formation over others listed here.
